_comment: DURABLE home-edge persist. The home Caddy boots from /etc/caddy/Caddyfile (--adapter caddyfile, NO --resume), so admin-API writes are EPHEMERAL — wiped on a container restart. This config makes a write survive a restart by reconciling it into the on-disk boot Caddyfile, read-back-verified. The home edge is a TWO-CHANNEL reality: the Caddyfile lives on the LXC host (/etc/homeedge/caddy/Caddyfile, bind-mounted READ-ONLY into the container), while `caddy validate/reload/adapt` must run INSIDE the container. So `transport` (admin API) and `caddy_persist.caddy_command` both target the container; `caddy_persist.file_command`/`file_path` target the host that holds the file. Without `caddy_persist` the edge declares ephemeral-admin and a write warns it will not survive a restart.
